underlying: New Jersey U.S. Senate 2026 D-R margin in pp (Booker D incumbent)
method: Anchored on (a) Booker's 2020 +16.3 over Mehta, 2014 +13.6 over Bell, (b) NJ shifted right in 2024 — Harris +6 vs Biden +16 in 2020, (c) D-favorable midterm environment (D+3 to D+6 generic ballot), (d) weak GOP field — no top-tier challenger declared (Zdan, Tabor, Murphy, Lebovics — non-elected), (e) Booker has national profile and large fundraising advantage. Mu=+14 splits Booker's two prior marks slightly below 2020 to reflect NJ's R-trend partially offset by midterm dynamics. Sigma 6 (no head-to-head polls).
notes: Booker (D inc) is heavily favored but NJ's 2024 swing right (Harris +6 vs Biden +16) implies erosion vs his 2020 mark. Confidence LOW because (a) no public 2026 polling yet, (b) gubernatorial race volatility in NJ. Mu=+14 is modestly below his 2014/2020 performances. Tail risk: a strong GOP recruit (e.g., Ciattarelli) enters and compresses to ~+5; current field is weak.
